requirementCueLines: Suggested First- and Second-Year Courses: English composition; ATMOS 220; MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126; PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123. The first upper-division core course is ATMOS 301, which is offered autumn quarter only. Any lower-division atmospheric sciences courses except ATMOS 220 count as electives and not as part of the major.
Admission Requirements
Minimum course requirements: English composition; MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126 (or MATH 134, MATH 135, MATH 136); PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123 (or PHYS 141, PHYS 142, PHYS 143)
Grade requirements: Minimum 2.0 grade for each course required for admission; minimum 2.50 cumulative GPA in courses required for admission

The Chemistry option provides students with a strong background in environmental issues such as evolving atmospheric composition and air quality through coursework that focuses on chemistry and the environment.
Completion Requirements
Minimum 85-98 credits, depending on credential/option
Foundation requirements (30 credits): MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126 (or MATH 134, MATH 135, MATH 136); PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123 (or PHYS 141, PHYS 142, PHYS 143)
Core requirements (27-28 credits): STAT 390 (or Q SCI 381 for options in meteorology, climate, chemistry); ATMOS 220 (1 credit), ATMOS 301, ATMOS 321, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 341, ATMOS 370, ATMOS 431.
Additional requirements specified below
The following elective course sequence is suitable preparation for students interested in pursuing graduate study in atmospheric sciences: ATMOS 301, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 441.
Additional Completion Requirements
Option specific credits (29-37 credits)

Requirements (23-27 credits): ATMOS 458/CHEM 458; CEE 480/ATMOS 480; ATMOS 310 or CSE 160; one of the following: (1) CHEM 142, CHEM 152, CHEM 162; (2) CHEM 143 and CHEM 153; (3)CHEM 145, CHEM 155, CHEM 165
Electives (6-10 credits): minimum two courses from an approved list. See adviser for approved list of electives.
The Climate Option provides students with a strong interdisciplinary ground in environmental change through the lens of atmospheric, ocean and earth sciences.
Option specific credits (31-40 credits)
Requirements (22-25 credits): ATMOS 350; ATMOS 358, ATMOS 380, ATMOS 487; ATMOS 310 or CSE 160; ESS 431 or ESS 433; OCEAN 423 or OCEAN 450
Electives (9-15 credits): minimum three courses from an approved list. See adviser for approved list of electives.
The Data Science Option provides students with a strong foundation in data science skills with specific applications to meteorology-, climate-, and air pollution-related careers.
Option specific credits (28-39 credits)
Requirements (28-39 credits):
one of CSE 123, CSE 143, or CSE 163
one of CSE 416/STAT 416, STAT 435, INFO 371, or ESS 469
SOC 225 (3 credits)
one of ATMOS 310, ATMOS 350, ATMOS 358, ATMOS 380, or ATMOS 458/CHEM 458
one of the following options: (1) MATH 207, MATH 208, MATH 209; (2) AMATH 351 and AMATH 353
CSE 414 or INFO 430
one of CSE 412, HCDE 411, INFO 474, or STAT 451
Note: Prerequisites for option specific courses could add up to 0-29 credits. See adviser for list of prerequisites.
The meteorology option provides students with a strong background in atmospheric dynamics, synoptic meteorology and weather forecasting.
Option specific credits (33-40 credits)
Requirements (23-24 credits): ATMOS 358, ATMOS 441, ATMOS 442, ATMOS 451, ATMOS 452; ATMOS 310 or CSE 160
Advanced Mathematics (10-16 credits): MATH 224; either AMATH 351 and AMATH 353, or MATH 207, MATH 208, and MATH 209

requirementCueLines: The Chemistry option provides students with a strong background in environmental issues such as evolving atmospheric composition and air quality through coursework that focuses on chemistry and the environment.
Completion Requirements
Minimum 85-98 credits, depending on credential/option
Foundation requirements (30 credits): MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126 (or MATH 134, MATH 135, MATH 136); PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123 (or PHYS 141, PHYS 142, PHYS 143)
Core requirements (27-28 credits): STAT 390 (or Q SCI 381 for options in meteorology, climate, chemistry); ATMOS 220 (1 credit), ATMOS 301, ATMOS 321, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 341, ATMOS 370, ATMOS 431.
Additional requirements specified below
The following elective course sequence is suitable preparation for students interested in pursuing graduate study in atmospheric sciences: ATMOS 301, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 441.
Additional Completion Requirements
Option specific credits (29-37 credits)

Requirements (23-27 credits): ATMOS 458/CHEM 458; CEE 480/ATMOS 480; ATMOS 310 or CSE 160; one of the following: (1) CHEM 142, CHEM 152, CHEM 162; (2) CHEM 143 and CHEM 153; (3)CHEM 145, CHEM 155, CHEM 165
Electives (6-10 credits): minimum two courses from an approved list. See adviser for approved list of electives.

requirementCueLines: Suggested First- and Second-Year Courses: English composition; ATMOS 220; MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126; PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123. The first upper-division core course is ATMOS 301, which is offered autumn quarter only. Any lower-division atmospheric sciences courses except ATMOS 220 count as electives and not as part of the major.
Admission Requirements
Minimum course requirements: English composition; MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126 (or MATH 134, MATH 135, MATH 136); PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123 (or PHYS 141, PHYS 142, PHYS 143)
Grade requirements: Minimum 2.0 grade for each course required for admission; minimum 2.50 cumulative GPA in courses required for admission

The Chemistry option provides students with a strong background in environmental issues such as evolving atmospheric composition and air quality through coursework that focuses on chemistry and the environment.
Completion Requirements
Minimum 85-98 credits, depending on credential/option
Foundation requirements (30 credits): MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126 (or MATH 134, MATH 135, MATH 136); PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123 (or PHYS 141, PHYS 142, PHYS 143)
Core requirements (27-28 credits): STAT 390 (or Q SCI 381 for options in meteorology, climate, chemistry); ATMOS 220 (1 credit), ATMOS 301, ATMOS 321, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 341, ATMOS 370, ATMOS 431.
Additional requirements specified below
The following elective course sequence is suitable preparation for students interested in pursuing graduate study in atmospheric sciences: ATMOS 301, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 441.

sourceLineHints: Suggested First- and Second-Year Courses: English composition; ATMOS 220; MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126; PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123. The first upper-division core course is ATMOS 301, which is offered autumn quarter only. Any lower-division atmospheric sciences courses except ATMOS 220 count as electives and not as part of the major.
Admission Requirements
Minimum course requirements: English composition; MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126 (or MATH 134, MATH 135, MATH 136); PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123 (or PHYS 141, PHYS 142, PHYS 143)
Grade requirements: Minimum 2.0 grade for each course required for admission; minimum 2.50 cumulative GPA in courses required for admission

requirementCueLines: The Data Science Option provides students with a strong foundation in data science skills with specific applications to meteorology-, climate-, and air pollution-related careers.
Completion Requirements
Minimum 85-98 credits, depending on credential/option
Foundation requirements (30 credits): MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126 (or MATH 134, MATH 135, MATH 136); PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123 (or PHYS 141, PHYS 142, PHYS 143)
Core requirements (27-28 credits): STAT 390 (or Q SCI 381 for options in meteorology, climate, chemistry); ATMOS 220 (1 credit), ATMOS 301, ATMOS 321, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 341, ATMOS 370, ATMOS 431.
Additional requirements specified below
The following elective course sequence is suitable preparation for students interested in pursuing graduate study in atmospheric sciences: ATMOS 301, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 441.
Additional Completion Requirements
Option specific credits (28-39 credits)
Requirements (28-39 credits):
one of CSE 123, CSE 143, or CSE 163
one of CSE 416/STAT 416, STAT 435, INFO 371, or ESS 469
SOC 225 (3 credits)
one of ATMOS 310, ATMOS 350, ATMOS 358, ATMOS 380, or ATMOS 458/CHEM 458
one of the following options: (1) MATH 207, MATH 208, MATH 209; (2) AMATH 351 and AMATH 353
CSE 414 or INFO 430
one of CSE 412, HCDE 411, INFO 474, or STAT 451
Note: Prerequisites for option specific courses could add up to 0-29 credits. See adviser for list of prerequisites.

parsedDegreeMapBlockCandidates.title: Atmospheric and Climate Science - B.S. Meteorology option parsed official source requirements
sourceLineHints: The meteorology option provides students with a strong background in atmospheric dynamics, synoptic meteorology and weather forecasting.
Completion Requirements
Minimum 85-98 credits, depending on credential/option
Foundation requirements (30 credits): MATH 124, MATH 125, MATH 126 (or MATH 134, MATH 135, MATH 136); PHYS 121, PHYS 122, PHYS 123 (or PHYS 141, PHYS 142, PHYS 143)
Core requirements (27-28 credits): STAT 390 (or Q SCI 381 for options in meteorology, climate, chemistry); ATMOS 220 (1 credit), ATMOS 301, ATMOS 321, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 341, ATMOS 370, ATMOS 431.
Additional requirements specified below
The following elective course sequence is suitable preparation for students interested in pursuing graduate study in atmospheric sciences: ATMOS 301, ATMOS 340, ATMOS 441.
Additional Completion Requirements
Option specific credits (33-40 credits)
Requirements (23-24 credits): ATMOS 358, ATMOS 441, ATMOS 442, ATMOS 451, ATMOS 452; ATMOS 310 or CSE 160
